✦ Module 1 ✦

🌟

The Fool's Leap

Introduction & Foundations

Begin your tarot journey with an open heart and curious mind. The Fool steps off the cliff not in ignorance, but in radical trust. That is where we begin — with beginner's mind, infinite possibility, and the courage to not know.

✦ Learning Objectives

  • ✦ Understand the structure of a tarot deck (78 cards, Major + Minor Arcana)
  • ✦ Learn card anatomy: imagery, symbolism, numerology, and suit correspondences
  • ✦ Establish your personal tarot practice — your space, your rituals, your intentions
  • ✦ Complete your first intuitive card pull without referencing any guidebook

What Is Tarot?

Tarot is not a crystal ball. It is not a device for predicting a fixed future. Tarot is a mirror — a sophisticated system of symbolism that reflects back the patterns, stories, and truths already living within you.

A tarot deck contains 78 cards, divided into the Major Arcana (22 cards representing universal life themes and archetypal forces) and the Minor Arcana (56 cards representing the everyday situations and experiences of human life across four suits).

The Structure of the Deck

Major Arcana (22 cards)

The big life lessons and spiritual themes. Numbered 0 (The Fool) through 21 (The World), they map the Fool's Journey — the soul's path through life.

Minor Arcana (56 cards)

The everyday experiences of life across four suits: Wands (fire/passion), Cups (water/emotion), Swords (air/thought), and Pentacles (earth/matter).

Card Anatomy

Every tarot card is a world unto itself. When you approach a card, observe: the imagery and symbols, the colors and their emotional tone, the number and its numerological significance, the figures and their posture, expression, and direction of gaze, and the background — sky, landscape, architecture.

Your first impression of a card — before you look up a single meaning — is data. Trust it.

Establishing Your Practice

A tarot practice is personal. There is no "right" way to do it. But here are the foundations of a meaningful practice:

🕯️

Create a Sacred Space

Light a candle, clear a small area, arrange items that feel meaningful. Signal to yourself that this is special time.

🌙

Set an Intention

Before you pull any card, ask a question or set an intention. 'What do I need to know today?' is always a good place to start.

🤲

Shuffle Intentionally

Shuffle while holding your question. Some shuffle seven times, some shuffle until it 'feels right'. Trust your instincts.

📓

Journal Your Readings

Keep a tarot journal. Date your pulls, note the card, and record your intuitive response before looking at any reference material.

🎯 Activity: Fearless First Pull

Go to the Card of the Day page and pull a card. Before reading any description, write in your journal:

  1. What is your first feeling or impression?
  2. What symbols catch your eye?
  3. What story does the image tell you?
  4. How does this card relate to your current life?

Then — and only then — read the card's meaning and reflection prompt.

📓 Worksheet: My Tarot Origin Story

Head to the Worksheets page to find your Module 1 worksheet. Reflect on:

  • ✦ What drew you to tarot?
  • ✦ What do you hope to learn?
  • ✦ What fears or doubts do you carry into this journey?

💬 Discussion Prompts

  • Q: What drew you to tarot? What do you hope to learn?
  • Q: How do you feel about intuition as a tool for insight?
  • Q: What is your relationship with symbolism and imagery?
